title: Amartya Sen’s capability approach and social justice in education, edited by M. Walker and E. Unterhalter, Basingstoke, Palgrave Macmillan, 2010, 292 pp., £19.99, ISBN 978-0-23-010459-4
abstract: Click to increase image sizeClick to decrease image size Notes 1. See Lessman, Otto, and Zielger (Citation2011) and Hart (Citation2012) for further reading on education, social justice and the CA. 2. HDCA website: www.capabilityapproach.com. At the time of writing (December 2011) the education thematic group was in the process of updating the bibliography, which was last revised in 2005. 3. See Deneulin and Shahani (Citation2009) and Agarwal, Humphries, and Robeyns (Citation2005) for further introduction and background to the CA. 4. For further developments in the field, see Studies in Philosophy & Education (Hinchcliffe and Terzi 2009) and also Prospero (Hinchcliffe Citation2007). 5. Unterhalter (Citation2003) has touched on this subject with her account of the vulnerability and abuse of girls in some schooling in South Africa. 6. See Deneulin, Nebel, and Sagovsky (Citation2006). 7. For contrasting views on the idea of autonomy, see Hand (Citation2006). 8. See, for example, Biggeri, Ballet, and Comim (Citation2011). The HDCA also have a children's thematic group, and details of researchers and publication in this field can be found via the HDCA website (www.capabilityapproach.org). 9. See Comim, Qizilbash, and Alkire (Citation2008) for further reading on measures and applications of the CA. 10. See Elster (Citation1983) and Bridges (Citation2006) on adapted preferences.
authors_text: Hart, Caroline Sarojini; Sarangapani, Padma M.; Lowe, John
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
